name: National Book Festival
description: Library of Congress flagship festival at the Walter E. Washington Convention Center; free, one-day celebration of reading.

name: LA Times Festival of Books
description: The nation's largest public book festival, held on the USC campus over two days with hundreds of authors and exhibitors.

name: Miami Book Fair
description: Eight-day fair at Miami Dade College drawing 550+ authors from 35+ countries; one of the most important literary events in the Americas.

name: Texas Book Festival
description: Annual two-day festival around the Texas State Capitol featuring 250+ authors, a street fair, and a Saturday Lit Crawl.

name: Southern Festival of Books
description: Free two-day festival at Bicentennial Mall and the Tennessee State Museum celebrating Southern literature and authors.

name: Brooklyn Book Festival
description: New York City's largest free literary event, spanning nine days with 500+ authors, the East Coast's biggest book market, and five boroughs.

name: Tucson Festival of Books
description: Free two-day festival on the University of Arizona campus with 300+ authors, science exhibitions, and family programming.

name: Printers Row Lit Fest
description: Free outdoor book fair in Chicago's historic Printers Row neighborhood; the Midwest's premier street literary festival.

name: Savannah Book Festival
description: Four-day festival in historic downtown Savannah with 40+ authors presenting at venues surrounding Telfair and Chippewa Squares.

name: Baltimore Book Festival
description: Free two-day festival in Baltimore's Waverly neighborhood featuring 80+ authors, six stages, literary panels, and youth programming.

name: Decatur Book Festival
description: Free two-day festival in downtown Decatur with 100+ national and local authors, a street fair, and cooking demonstrations.

name: Virginia Festival of the Book
description: Four-day literary festival at the University of Virginia celebrating authors, ideas, and the literary arts across Charlottesville.

name: ThrillerFest
description: Annual conference of the International Thriller Writers at the New York Hilton Midtown; workshops, panels, and the ITW Award ceremony.

name: Bouchercon World Mystery Convention
description: The world's largest mystery fan convention, rotating cities annually; the 2025 edition returns to New Orleans in September.
recurringPattern: Annual – rotating city, typically late August/September

name: Portland Book Festival
description: Annual one-day festival (formerly Wordstock) across ten stages in Portland's South Park Blocks, presented by Literary Arts.

name: Litquake / Lit Crawl SF
description: SF's two-week literary festival culminating in Lit Crawl, the world's largest free pop-up lit event with 250+ authors roaming the Mission District.

name: Nantucket Book Festival
description: Four-day island literary festival with free public events featuring bestselling and Pulitzer Prize-winning authors each June.

name: New Orleans Book Festival at Tulane
description: Free four-day festival on Tulane University's uptown campus with 90+ panels, culinary symposia, and family programming.

name: Boston Book Festival
description: Free one-day festival in Copley Square with 200+ authors presenting across the Boston Public Library and surrounding historic churches.

name: Louisville Book Festival
description: Free one-day festival at the Kentucky International Convention Center with 100+ authors celebrating reading and storytelling.

name: PEN World Voices Festival
description: PEN America's international literary festival celebrating writers from 35+ countries with events in Manhattan's Greenwich Village.
recurringPattern: Annual – late April to early May

name: Twin Cities Book Festival
description: Rain Taxi's annual all-day book fair at Union Depot with 130+ publishers, literary organizations, and author events for all ages.

name: Kentucky Book Festival
description: Free all-day festival at Joseph-Beth Booksellers in Lexington with 150+ authors celebrating books and literacy in the Commonwealth.

name: Harlem Book Fair
description: The nation's largest African American book fair on 135th Street in Harlem, celebrating Black literature, culture, and intellectual life each July.

name: NYC Poetry Festival
description: Free two-day outdoor poetry festival on Governors Island featuring 200+ poets, publishers, and literary magazines in a stunning harbor setting.

name: Chautauqua Literary Arts Festival
description: Summer-long literary arts programming at the historic Chautauqua Institution, including author readings, the Chautauqua Prize, and writing workshops.

name: Vermont Book Festival
description: Multi-day festival celebrating Vermont authors and the state's vibrant literary culture with readings, panels, and a bustling book fair in Burlington.

name: New Hampshire Book Festival
description: Annual two-day celebration of New Hampshire authors and literature, with panel discussions, readings, and a book fair at Concord venues.

name: History Book Festival
description: Free annual festival at Legislative Mall in Dover with Delaware authors and national guests, presented by the Delaware Division of Libraries.

name: Dodge Poetry Festival
description: The largest poetry event in North America, held biennially at NJPAC in Newark with 100+ poets, 18,000+ attendees, and four days of readings and workshops.

name: Malice Domestic
description: Annual mystery fan convention in suburban DC celebrating the traditional mystery, honoring the Agatha Awards, and connecting readers with authors.

name: Split This Rock Poetry Festival
description: Biennial festival in Washington, DC celebrating socially engaged poetry; features 200+ poets, workshops, and the Poem of the Week gallery.

name: Tennessee Williams/New Orleans Literary Festival
description: Five-day festival in the French Quarter honoring the playwright's legacy with author readings, a Stanley and Stella shouting contest, and literary walking tours.

name: Mississippi Book Festival
description: Free one-day literary lawn party on the grounds of the Mississippi State Capitol celebrating the state's rich storytelling tradition.

name: Oxford Conference for the Book
description: Three-day academic and popular literary conference at the University of Mississippi exploring books, publishing, and Southern literature.

name: Arkansas Book Festival
description: Free one-day festival at the Arkansas Studies Institute and Fulbright Library celebrating Arkansas authors and readers across all genres.

name: North Carolina Literary Festival
description: Biennial statewide celebration of North Carolina writers hosted by UNC-Chapel Hill, featuring readings, workshops, and a book fair across the university campus.

name: Tallahassee Book Festival
description: Free one-day outdoor festival on the historic Kleman Plaza featuring Florida authors, storytelling for children, poetry slams, and independent booksellers.

name: Jacksonville Book Festival
description: Multi-day literary celebration in downtown Jacksonville featuring panel discussions, author signings, and events at the Jacksonville Main Library.

name: Pensacola Book Festival
description: Annual free festival at the Pensacola Bay Center celebrating Gulf Coast authors and literacy with readings, booksellers, and family programming.

name: West Virginia Book Festival
description: Three-day festival at the Charleston Civic Center celebrating Appalachian and national literature with author readings, a book sale, and children's events.

name: Thomas Wolfe Festival
description: Annual October festival in Asheville honoring native son Thomas Wolfe with readings at the Thomas Wolfe Memorial, author events, and literary tours.

name: Carolina Mountains Literary Festival
description: Intimate three-day literary festival in the Blue Ridge Mountains of western NC, featuring 30+ authors in a beautiful small-town Appalachian setting.

name: Wisconsin Book Festival
description: Five-day free literary festival hosted by the Madison Public Library featuring 100+ authors across fiction, memoir, poetry, and children's literature.

name: Iowa City Book Festival
description: Annual fall festival in the UNESCO City of Literature celebrating the written word with author events across Iowa City's independent bookshops and cultural venues.

name: Festival of Faith & Writing
description: Biennial gathering at Calvin University exploring the intersection of literature and faith with 100+ authors including Pulitzer and National Book Award winners.

name: South Dakota Festival of Books
description: Annual three-day free literary festival presented by South Dakota Humanities Council at South Dakota State University with 50+ authors and family programming.

name: Kansas Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival at the Kansas State Capitol and Topeka & Shawnee County Public Library celebrating Kansas authors and literary culture.

name: Southern Kentucky Book Fest
description: Annual free book festival at the Knicely Conference Center in Bowling Green, featuring 100+ authors and presented by the Warren County Public Library.

name: San Antonio Book Festival
description: Free one-day festival at the Central Library and Travis Park with 150+ authors celebrating San Antonio's diverse literary culture and Texas storytelling.

name: North Texas Book Festival
description: Annual free fall festival at Denton's Emily Fowler Central Library celebrating North Texas authors, independent publishers, and family reading.

name: El Paso Book Festival
description: Bilingual literary celebration on the US-Mexico border showcasing Borderlands authors in English and Spanish, with a focus on border culture and identity.

name: Albuquerque Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival celebrating the diverse literary voices of New Mexico and the Southwest, held at the Albuquerque Convention Center.

name: Utah Humanities Book Festival
description: Annual statewide literary celebration presented by Utah Humanities, with author events at libraries and cultural venues across Salt Lake City and Utah.

name: Nevada Humanities Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival presented by Nevada Humanities at the National Automobile Museum in Reno celebrating Nevada authors and the literary arts.

name: Bay Area Book Festival
description: Free two-day festival in downtown Berkeley bringing together 300+ authors, 100+ exhibitors, and 30,000+ book lovers for the West Coast's premier literary celebration.

name: San Diego Festival of Books
description: Annual free literary festival celebrating San Diego authors and the region's diverse reading culture at the San Diego Library and Balboa Park venues.

name: Sacramento Book Fair
description: Annual gathering of independent booksellers, small presses, and literary organizations celebrating books and reading culture in California's capital.

name: Seattle Book Festival
description: Annual literary event hosted by Hugo House across Seattle venues featuring Northwest authors, publisher showcases, and an inclusive celebration of Seattle's reading culture.

name: Locus Awards Weekend
description: Annual science fiction and fantasy awards weekend presenting the Locus Awards, voted on by readers of Locus Magazine; includes panels, readings, and a gala ceremony.

name: Hawaii Book & Music Festival
description: Annual free outdoor festival on the grounds of Honolulu Hale celebrating Pacific Island literature, local authors, and Hawaii's unique multicultural storytelling traditions.

name: Left Coast Crime
description: Annual mystery fan convention rotating among West Coast cities, celebrating crime fiction with author panels, awards, and the Lefty Award ceremony.
recurringPattern: Annual – March/April, rotating West Coast cities

name: World Fantasy Convention
description: Annual fantasy and horror literature convention rotating US and international cities, presenting the World Fantasy Awards and gathering the genre's top authors and editors.

name: Nebula Awards Conference
description: Annual Science Fiction and Fantasy Writers of America conference presenting the Nebula Awards, rotating among US cities; includes craft panels and an awards banquet.

name: Romance Writers of America Conference
description: Annual conference of romance writers presenting the RITA Awards and Golden Heart Awards, with agent and editor appointments and craft workshops.

name: Houston Poetry Fest
description: Annual two-day festival at Evelyn's Park Conservancy celebrating poetry with 100+ poets, open-mic events, slam competitions, and readings for all ages.

name: Nebraska Book Festival
description: Annual literary celebration presented by Nebraska Humanities at the Nebraska State Capitol celebrating Nebraska authors and the state's literary heritage.

name: North Dakota Humanities Book Festival
description: Annual literary event presented by North Dakota Humanities celebrating Great Plains authors, indigenous storytellers, and regional literary voices.

name: Heartland Book Festival
description: Annual celebration of authors and books in Kansas City, featuring Midwest writers and national guests at the Kansas City Public Library's central branch.

name: Richmond Book Arts Festival
description: Annual celebration of book arts, handmade books, and literary culture at the Valentine Museum in Richmond, with printmakers, bookbinders, and authors.

name: Nashville Book Festival
description: Annual free one-day event at the Nashville Public Library celebrating books and reading with Nashville authors, national guests, and family programming.

name: Atlanta Book Fair at MOCA GA
description: Annual free book fair at the Museum of Contemporary Art of Georgia celebrating Atlanta's independent publishers, small presses, and literary community.

name: Columbus Book Festival
description: Annual literary celebration at COSI and the Columbus Metropolitan Library celebrating books, authors, and Ohio's reading community.

name: Pittsburgh Contemporary Writers Series
description: Annual literary celebration at Carnegie Mellon University and across Pittsburgh venues featuring national and regional authors in fiction, poetry, and nonfiction.

name: Seattle Lit Crawl
description: Annual one-night literary pub crawl through Seattle's Capitol Hill neighborhood with dozens of readings in bars, restaurants, and venues; part of the Lit Crawl network.

name: Chicago Humanities Festival
description: Annual multi-week public festival presenting the world's leading authors, thinkers, and artists at venues across Chicago including Millennium Park and the Chicago Cultural Center.

name: O, Miami Poetry Festival
description: Annual month-long April celebration placing a poem in front of every Miami-Dade County resident, with 200+ events in parks, galleries, buses, and public spaces.

name: New Mexico Book Fiesta
description: Annual free outdoor literary festival on the Santa Fe Plaza celebrating New Mexico's rich literary tradition, Southwest authors, and bilingual storytelling.

name: Oxford Literary Festival at Ole Miss
description: Annual spring literary weekend in Oxford, MS — Faulkner's hometown — celebrating Southern writing with author talks, square strolling, and Square Books events.

name: Boulder Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival at the Boulder Public Library and downtown venues celebrating Colorado authors and the Front Range's vibrant literary community.

name: Mission Creek Festival
description: Annual spring festival in Iowa City blending literature, music, and film across bars, bookstores, and cultural venues in the heart of this UNESCO City of Literature.

name: South Texas Literary Festival
description: Annual celebration of South Texas and border-region authors at Texas A&M University-Corpus Christi, featuring bilingual literature and Gulf Coast storytelling.

name: Spokane Is Reading
description: Annual citywide reading festival in Spokane presenting author events at the Spokane Public Library, bookshops, and cultural venues across the Inland Northwest.

name: Oklahoma Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival at the State Capitol complex celebrating Oklahoma authors and literacy with readings, workshops, and a book signing fair.

name: Fayetteville Roots Festival
description: Annual roots music and literary arts festival in Fayetteville pairing Southern authors and storytellers with the Ozark mountain music tradition.

name: Black Hills Literary Festival
description: Annual literary gathering in historic Deadwood, South Dakota celebrating Western American literature, Native American storytelling, and frontier literary traditions.

name: Word Festival
description: Annual three-day literary festival in the pastoral village of Cooperstown, NY featuring award-winning authors in an intimate setting alongside the Baseball Hall of Fame.

name: Wichita Literary Festival
description: Annual free literary celebration at Wichita Public Library featuring Kansas authors, national guests, and programming for readers of all ages across the city.

name: Vermont Humanities Festival
description: Annual statewide humanities celebration hosted by Vermont Humanities with author talks, book discussions, and literary events across Vermont communities.

name: Seattle Poetry Festival
description: Annual multi-day poetry celebration in Seattle featuring Northwest poets and national voices at venues across the city, celebrating Seattle's status as a poetry capital.

name: Roanoke Regional Book Festival
description: Annual literary celebration in Virginia's Blue Ridge mountain city featuring Appalachian authors and regional writers at the Taubman Museum of Art.

name: Orlando Book Festival
description: Annual literary event at the Orange County Library System celebrating Central Florida authors and offering free author talks, workshops, and a book fair.

name: San Francisco Literary Festival
description: Annual celebration of San Francisco's legendary literary culture at venues from North Beach to the Mission, honoring the city's Beat Generation legacy and contemporary voices.

name: Los Angeles Literary Festival
description: Annual fall celebration of LA's diverse literary voices at the Hammer Museum and Westside venues, separate from the spring Times Festival of Books.

name: Denver Art Book Fair
description: Annual gathering of independent publishers, zine makers, and art book creators at the Denver Art Museum celebrating artist books and literary art objects.

name: Maine Book Festival
description: Annual literary celebration on the Maine coast featuring Maine authors, small presses, and national voices at the Portland Public Library and Congress Street venues.

name: Wyoming Book Festival
description: Annual free literary festival presented by Wyoming Humanities at the Wyoming State Library, celebrating Western American literature and Wyoming's storied frontier heritage.

name: National Cowboy Poetry Gathering
description: Week-long celebration of Western heritage through cowboy poetry, music, storytelling, and crafts at the Western Folklife Center; the premier event of its kind since 1985.

name: Durango Cowboy Poetry Gathering
description: Annual four-day gathering in the mountain town of Durango celebrating cowboy poets, Western musicians, and the ranching culture of the Southwest.

name: Montana Cowboy Poetry Gathering
description: Annual summer gathering in the heart of Montana's cattle country, featuring cowboy poets, Western musicians, and storytellers from across the Northern Rockies.

name: Nebraska Sandhills Cowboy Poetry Gathering
description: Annual celebration of Great Plains ranch culture through cowboy poetry and Western music in the Sandhills of north-central Nebraska.

name: Alpine Cowboy Poetry Gathering
description: Annual cowboy poetry celebration at Sul Ross State University in the Big Bend region, honoring the ranch culture and storytelling traditions of the Trans-Pecos.

name: Arizona Cowboy Poets Gathering
description: Annual gathering in historic Prescott celebrating the cowboy poetry tradition of the Southwest, held in the town once called the 'Cowboy Capital of the World.'

name: Pendleton Cowboy Poetry Gathering
description: Annual gathering in Pendleton, Oregon — home of the famous Pendleton Round-Up — celebrating Northwest cowboy poetry and Western American storytelling traditions.

name: Idaho Cowboy Poetry Gathering
description: Annual Idaho gathering honoring the Basque and ranching heritage of the Treasure Valley through cowboy poetry, traditional music, and Western storytelling.

name: Austin International Poetry Festival
description: The world's largest non-competitive poetry festival, held annually in April in Austin with 500+ poets from 50+ countries reading at venues across the city.

name: Louder Than a Bomb
description: The world's largest youth poetry festival, run by Young Chicago Authors, featuring 120+ Chicago high school teams in a month-long celebration of spoken word poetry.

name: Brave New Voices International Teen Poetry Slam
description: Annual international teen slam poetry festival hosted by Youth Speaks, featuring young poets from cities worldwide competing and collaborating over a week-long event.

name: Women of the World Poetry Slam
description: Annual international women's slam poetry championship featuring women poets from 150+ cities competing over five days; the premier gathering of women's voices in slam poetry.

name: Nuyorican Poets Cafe Friday Night Slam
description: The birthplace of slam poetry — the legendary Nuyorican Poets Cafe on East 3rd Street hosts its iconic Friday Night Slam year-round, a landmark of spoken word culture since 1973.

name: Pittsburgh Poetry Festival
description: Annual free spring poetry celebration in Pittsburgh featuring local and national poets at Carnegie Library and venues across the Steel City.

name: Los Angeles Poetry Festival
description: Annual multi-day celebration of poetry in Los Angeles with readings, open mics, and workshops at venues across the city, honoring LA's diverse and vibrant poetry community.

name: Chicago Poetry Festival
description: Annual fall festival celebrating Chicago's rich poetry heritage with readings at bars, galleries, and cultural venues across the city's literary neighborhoods.

name: World Science Fiction Convention (Worldcon)
description: The world's oldest and most prestigious science fiction convention presenting the Hugo Awards, rotating among international cities each year since 1939.
recurringPattern: Annual – August, rotating US and international cities

name: StokerCon
description: Annual Horror Writers Association convention presenting the Bram Stoker Awards and gathering horror fiction writers, editors, and fans. Rotates among US cities.

name: Readercon
description: Annual literary science fiction convention near Boston focused on the literary aspects of speculative fiction; known for its intellectually rigorous panels and strong author presence.

name: Capclave
description: Annual small, literary-focused science fiction convention in the Washington DC area, known for its author-centric programming and the WSFA Small Press Award.

name: YALLfest
description: Annual free YA and middle-grade book festival in downtown Charleston with 70+ authors, thousands of fans, and a legendary signing line; one of the biggest YA events in the US.

name: YALLWest
description: Annual free West Coast YA and middle-grade book festival at the Santa Monica Civic Auditorium featuring 60+ authors, panels, and a massive book signing.

name: Balticon
description: Annual science fiction and fantasy convention presented by the Baltimore Science Fiction Society, with a strong literary track, readings, and the Compton Crook Award for debut SF novels.

name: New England Crime Bake
description: Annual mystery and thriller writers' conference near Boston presenting the Agatha Award nominations and offering workshops and agent/editor panels for crime writers.

name: Books by the Banks
description: Cincinnati's beloved family literary festival celebrating books for readers of all ages, with a special emphasis on children's and YA literature. Free admission, author signings, and readings by national bestselling authors.

name: Northern California Children's Book Festival
description: Annual festival celebrating children's literature in the San Francisco Bay Area, featuring beloved picture book authors and illustrators, storytelling performances, and literary activities for young readers and their families.

name: San Antonio Book Festival — ¡Viva Books! Children's Stage
description: The San Antonio Book Festival's bilingual children's programming celebrates English- and Spanish-language children's literature, featuring diverse authors and illustrators, storytelling, and a special focus on Latinx children's books.

name: Miami Book Fair — KidsFest
description: The children's programming weekend of Miami Book Fair International, one of the nation's largest book events, draws thousands of young readers for author meet-and-greets, interactive storytelling, and activities in both English and Spanish.

name: Feria del Libro de Puerto Rico
description: Puerto Rico's premier annual book fair held at the Capitol grounds in San Juan, celebrating Puerto Rican and Latin American literature with hundreds of publishers, authors, readings, workshops, and cultural events. One of the most important literary gatherings in the Caribbean.

name: Festival de la Palabra
description: International literary festival in Old San Juan celebrating Spanish-language and world literature with readings, conversations, and performances by internationally acclaimed writers from Puerto Rico, Latin America, Spain, and beyond.

name: Feria del Libro de Ponce
description: Regional book fair in Puerto Rico's second-largest city, celebrating local and Caribbean literature with author presentations, workshops for children and adults, and a vibrant community atmosphere in the Pearl of the South.

name: Festival Internacional de Poesía de Puerto Rico
description: International poetry festival gathering poets from Puerto Rico, Latin America, and the world for readings, workshops, and cultural exchanges in San Juan. Celebrates the rich oral and written poetry traditions of the Caribbean.

name: Rancho Mirage Writers' Fest
description: World-class gathering of authors and readers at the Rancho Mirage Library and Observatory. Founded in 2014 and described as 'Coachella for the brain.' Features 60 authors and 1000 readers.

name: Sunshine State Book Festival
description: Authors from around Florida and the nation gather to talk about their writing, sell, and sign books. Includes panel discussions and story time for kids.

name: Booksville Festival
description: Celebrates all the ways literature touches our lives. Features author readings, book sales, games, activities based on beloved books, character contests, and spelling bees.

name: JAX Urban Book Festival
description: Features author interviews, book signings, networking, kids activities with free books, and a silent auction. A celebration of literature and culture.

name: Bookworm Book Festival for Young Readers
description: A free bilingual book festival that celebrates emerging readers and those who create books for them.

name: Monroeville Literary Festival
description: Discover new authors, celebrate favorite writers and share great times with others who love Southern literature in the courtroom made famous by To Kill a Mockingbird.

name: Oahu Indie Book Festival
description: Meet authors and illustrators, buy signed books, and win giveaways. Learn about writing or publishing with small group sessions. Free, family friendly.

name: African American Children's Book Fair
description: One of the oldest and largest single-day events for children's books in the country. Features nationally known bestselling authors and illustrators.

name: Amelia Island Book Festival
description: Public events featuring New York Times-bestselling authors, independent publishers, awards programs and workshops. Engages young people and adults in the joy of reading.

name: Appalachian WordFest
description: Brings together authors, young poets, publishers, and book lovers to celebrate the words and creativity emerging from the Appalachian Mountains. Features Poetry Slam competition.

name: San Antonio African American Book Festival
description: A festival committed to celebrating the power of Black literature, reinforcing that representation matters, and encouraging Black economics.

name: Southwest Florida Reading Festival
description: A free event presented by the Lee County Library System to promote reading, literacy, and library services. Features author presentations, book signings, and family fun.

name: Dahlonega Literary Festival
description: An intimate annual celebration of books and authors held in historic downtown Dahlonega, featuring authors from a variety of fiction and nonfiction genres.

name: Revival: Lost Southern Voices
description: Celebrates historically excluded, erased, or marginalized Southern voices. Presenters discuss Southern authors whose works are out-of-print or underappreciated. 10th anniversary in 2026.

name: New Orleans Book Festival at Tulane University
description: Supports and nurtures a literary community by connecting readers of all ages with local and national authors through experiences that celebrate the power of literacy.

name: Tennessee Williams & New Orleans Literary Festival
description: More than just a book festival — includes writing workshops, culinary and cocktail events, theatre events, music events, and a special focus on the history and culture of New Orleans.

name: Saints & Sinners LGBTQ+ Literary Festival
description: Founded in 2003 to bring the LGBTQ+ literary community together to celebrate the literary arts. Features writers, thinkers, and spokespeople of the LGBTQ+ community.

name: Book Lovers Con
description: Immersive reader experiences, mingling with beloved storytellers, morning mixers, three-hour book fests, and unforgettable evening events.

name: Woodstock Book Fest
description: Annual literary festival celebrating books and storytelling with author talks, panel discussions, and the popular Bookfest Story Slam.

name: Lexington Book Festival
description: Connects readers and writers, developing a passion for literature in readers of all ages. A nonprofit celebrating books and introducing writers to the local community.

name: Lehigh Valley Book Festival
description: A celebration of books and the connections they forge — bringing published authors, illustrators, and readers through thoughtful and inclusive programming.

name: Williamsburg Book Festival
description: Family-friendly annual event bringing together authors, poets, speakers, illustrators, and publishers from Virginia and beyond.

name: Pasadena Festival of Women Authors
description: Annual literary event celebrating and showcasing the work of women writers. Features panel discussions, author talks, and book signings.

name: Los Angeles Times Festival of Books
description: One of the largest book festivals in the nation attracting 160000 people annually. Brings together the people who create books with the people who love to read them.

name: Sacramento Book Festival
description: Major literary event hosting more than 200 authors at the Scottish Rite Masonic Center. Features big name authors, prizes, food trucks, and outdoor seating.

name: BAM! Festival
description: A book, art, and music festival held annually in downtown West Palm Beach. Brings together authors, teachers, students, families, and the community to share their love of books.

name: Word of South: A Festival of Literature and Music
description: A unique blend of writers and musicians exploring the relationship between literature and music. Showcases authors who write about music and musicians who are also authors.

name: Books Along the Teche Literary Festival
description: A celebration of Southern writing and culture featuring authors, social events, panel discussions. Draws bibliophiles and lovers of Louisiana cuisine and music.

name: Newburyport Literary Festival
description: Annual festival bringing together readers and writers for a weekend of readings, panel discussions, and other book-related events.

name: Midwest Literary Walk
description: Author interviews and presentations hosted on the same day at different venues within walking distance. Celebration of literature, reading, and the Chelsea community.

name: Unbound Book Festival
description: Brings nationally and internationally recognized authors of world-class renown to Columbia to talk about their books, their work, and their lives.

name: Hamptons Whodunit
description: Long Island's premiere mystery and true crime festival where fact and fiction collide. Features bestselling authors, legal experts, graveyard tours, escape rooms, and more.

name: BookCon
description: A two-day event where storytelling and pop culture collide. Features intimate author chats, immersive experiences, book swaps, and activities for lovers of storytelling.

name: Teen Bookfest by the Bay
description: A free festival featuring middle grade and young adult authors that supports, promotes, and celebrates the joy of reading.

name: Get Lit at the Beach
description: One of only a handful of festivals existing solely for the pleasure of listening to writers talk about their work and sharing conversations about the importance of reading.

name: Get Lit! Literary Art Festival
description: Annual festival that has grown to attract 10000 people from Spokane and the surrounding region. Celebrates literature, reading, and the literary arts.

name: Greensboro Bound Literary Festival
description: Annual literary festival connecting readers and writers in Greensboro, North Carolina.

name: Conyers Book Festival
description: Annual book festival connecting readers and writers in Conyers, Georgia.

name: Annapolis Book Festival
description: Free community festival celebrating the written word for 23 years. Features nationally renowned authors, children's activities, live music, food, and a literary marketplace.

name: Gaithersburg Book Festival
description: One of the nation's top literary events. Attracts hundreds of award-winning and bestselling authors, poets, and songwriters from across the country.

name: Montclair Literary Festival
description: Community-wide event aiming to exchange ideas, inspire future literary works, and engage with different points of view. Partners with local library and bookstore.

name: Santa Fe Literary Festival
description: Connects writers, readers, and thinkers in the celebration of words and story. Fosters a love of storytelling among young readers and writers in a city with deep literary history.

name: Hudson Children's Book Festival
description: Free public festival in historic Hudson striving to create a culture of literacy. Families meet world-class creators of books for children of all ages.

name: Greater Pittsburgh Festival of Books
description: Free event celebrating Pittsburgh's rich literary community. Features local and nationally-recognized authors, poets, children's activities, bookstores, and entertainment.

name: Mountain Words Literary Festival
description: Multi-day literary celebration at 9000 feet featuring readings, workshops, kids events, parties, and panel discussions. Immersive mountain literary experience.

name: Sonoma Valley Authors Festival
description: Three-day weekend lecture series bringing people together to hear authors from a variety of literary genres and speakers discussing advances in science and medicine.

name: Imaginarium Book Festival
description: Features 80+ author and vendor exhibitors, book signings, and special events. Celebrates fantasy, young adult, and genre fiction.

name: Sunbelt Book & Art Expo
description: Where stories, art, and creativity unite. Features book signings, conversations, panels, presentations, visual artists, and live art demonstrations.

name: National Book Club Conference
description: The premier annual literary event featuring Black authors and readers, called 'Literary Bliss.' A reader's paradise with unprecedented access to acclaimed literary talent.

name: Black Authors Festival
description: USA Today Top Festival celebrating Black literary excellence. Set in the heart of Sag Harbor with aspiring and established Black authors. Theme: The Right to Write.

name: Louisiana Book Festival
description: Annual celebration of Louisiana literature and reading culture held in Baton Rouge.

name: LitFest in the Dena
description: Free community literary festival founded in 2012 connecting Pasadena, Altadena, and greater Los Angeles with local authors through panels, readings, workshops, and literary performances.

name: Arizona Author Book Festival
description: Showcases 40+ Arizona authors across all genres, with a dedicated neurodivergent/autistic authors section, author talks, and book signings.

name: Bookstock
description: Award-winning authors, distinguished poets, a famous used book sale, children's programming, master classes, and the Bookstock Market in the heart of Vermont.

name: Cape Cod Book Festival
description: Held at the Cotuit Center for the Arts, a new cultural footprint in Massachusetts where charitably minded readers can meet great writers and thinkers — welcoming to locals and 'washashores' alike.

name: Ann Arbor Antiquarian Book Fair
description: Nearly five decades of rare and antiquarian booksellers from across America, dealing in old and rare books, Americana, children's books, autographs, manuscripts, maps, prints, ephemera, photography, and fine press material.

name: Maine Lit Fest
description: A week-long, statewide celebration of books and stories produced by Maine Writers & Publishers Alliance in collaboration with community partners and volunteers.

name: Santa Barbara Literary Festival
description: A two-day downtown celebration bringing together readers and writers of every genre through panels, workshops, pop-up events, and social gatherings in an intimate setting that fosters community around a shared love of the literary arts.

name: Shreve Memorial Library Children's Book Festival
description: A family-friendly, daylong literary celebration featuring children's book authors and illustrators, costumed characters, and interactive games and activities for children and families.
